tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: And having bound the strong one
sm: and having bound the strong one,

syn.mc: Coordinate participial member continuing the self-identification from lines 818-820.
syn.svo: Participle: δήσας, coordinated with the prior participles. Object: τὸν ἰσχυρόν.
syn.ptc: δήσας remains appositional to ἐγώ from line 818.
syn.rhet: The victory catalogue now takes up the binding of the strong adversary.

src: καὶ ἀφαρπάσας τὸν ἄνθρωπον εἰς τὰ ὑψηλὰ τῶν οὐρανῶν"
ana: καὶ ἀφαρπάσας τὸν ἄνθρωπον εἰς τὰ ὑψηλὰ τῶν οὐρανῶν,
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: And having snatched the human being into the heights of the heavens,
sm: and having carried humanity off to the heights of heaven,

syn.mc: Coordinate participial member completed by line 823.
syn.svo: Participle: ἀφαρπάσας. Object: τὸν ἄνθρωπον. Goal phrase: εἰς τὰ ὑψηλὰ τῶν οὐρανῶν.
syn.ptc: ἀφαρπάσας continues the victorious appositional catalogue.
syn.rhet: The bound enemy is paired with humanity's exaltation.

lit: I, says Christ.
sm: It is I, says Christ.

syn.mc: Speech-tag closure for the self-identification sequence begun at line 818.
syn.svo: Quoted subject: ἐγώ. Parenthetical verb: φησίν. Speaker identification: ὁ Χριστός.
syn.app: ὁ Χριστός identifies the speaker after φησίν.
syn.rhet: The victorious catalogue resolves into explicit christological self-naming.

src: τοίνυν δεῦτε πᾶσαι αἱ πατριαὶ τῶν ἀνθρώπων
lit: Therefore come, all the families of human beings,
sm: Therefore come, all families of mankind,

syn.sub: The participial qualification follows in line 825; the second imperative follows in line 826.
syn.svo: Imperative: δεῦτε. Vocative-like subject phrase: πᾶσαι αἱ πατριαὶ τῶν ἀνθρώπων.
syn.rhet: The speech widens from victory claim to universal invitation.

tok: pf mp ptcp nom pl f
lit: Those having been mixed in sins
sm: you who are steeped in sins,

syn.mc: Attributive participial qualification of the addressees from line 824.
syn.svo: Participle: πεφυραμέναι, modifying αἱ πατριαί. Prepositional phrase: ἐν ἁμαρτίαις.
syn.ptc: πεφυραμέναι is attributive and characterizes the condition of the invited hearers.
syn.rhet: Universal address is sharpened by a stark diagnosis of sin.

lit: And receive forgiveness of sins.
sm: and receive forgiveness of sins.

syn.mc: Second imperative addressed to the hearers.
syn.svo: Imperative: λάβετε. Object: ἄφεσιν ἁμαρτημάτων.
syn.rhet: The invitation culminates immediately in the gift offered.

src: ἐγὼ γάρ εἰμι ὑμῶν ἡ ἄφεσις,
lit: For I am your forgiveness,
sm: For I am your forgiveness,

syn.mc: Main declarative clause grounding the invitation.
syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Verb: εἰμι. Predicate nominative: ἡ ἄφεσις. Possessive genitive: ὑμῶν.
syn.rhet: The speaker identifies himself not only as giver but as forgiveness itself.

lit: I, the Passover of salvation,
sm: I am the Passover of salvation,

syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Predicate nominal phrase: τὸ πάσχα τῆς σωτηρίας, with εἰμι implied from line 827.
syn.app: The nominal phrase stands in apposition/predication to ἐγώ.
syn.rhet: Passover imagery is directly absorbed into Christ's self-definition.

src: ἐγὼ ὁ ἀμνὸς ὃ ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν σφαγείς"
ana: ἐγὼ ὁ ἀμνὸς ὁ ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν σφαγείς·
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: I, the lamb, the one slain for you;
sm: I am the lamb slain for you;
emd: The raw ὃ is corrected to ὁ to restore the articular participial phrase after ἀμνός.

syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Predicate phrase: ὁ ἀμνός, further defined by ὁ ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν σφαγείς.
syn.ptc: σφαγείς is attributive within the predicate nominal phrase.
syn.app: ὁ ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν σφαγείς apposes and specifies ὁ ἀμνός.
syn.rhet: The central sacrificial image is named explicitly.

lit: I, your ransom,
sm: I am your ransom,

syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Predicate nominal: τὸ λύτρον ὑμῶν, with εἰμι implied.
syn.app: The nominal phrase stands in apposition/predication to ἐγώ.
syn.rhet: Sacrificial imagery gives way to redemption language.

lit: I, your life,
sm: I am your life,

syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Predicate nominal: ἡ ζωὴ ὑμῶν.
syn.app: The nominal phrase stands in apposition/predication to ἐγώ.
syn.rhet: The series compresses salvation into a sequence of absolute predicates.

lit: <UNCERTAIN: pure OCR noise line>
sm: <UNCERTAIN: omitted as pure OCR noise>
emd: Followed change_log.txt: τιν κα Ια τάκ ἃ -> [DELETED]. The line contains no recoverable Greek syntax.

syn.svo: None; pure OCR debris.
syn.rhet: None; excluded from continuous syntax.

lit: I, your light,
sm: I am your light,

syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Predicate nominal: τὸ φῶς ὑμῶν.
syn.app: The nominal phrase stands in apposition/predication to ἐγώ.
syn.rhet: The sequence expands into Johannine-style light imagery.

lit: I, your salvation,
sm: I am your salvation,

syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Predicate nominal: ἡ σωτηρία ὑμῶν.
syn.app: The nominal phrase stands in apposition/predication to ἐγώ.
syn.rhet: The predicate restates the saving identity announced earlier.

lit: I, your resurrection,
sm: I am your resurrection,

syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Predicate nominal: ἡ ἀνάστασις ὑμῶν.
syn.app: The nominal phrase stands in apposition/predication to ἐγώ.
syn.rhet: Resurrection is now personalized in the speaker.

lit: I, your king;
sm: I am your king;

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal self-identification closing the predicate series.
syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Predicate nominal: ὁ βασιλεὺς ὑμῶν.
syn.app: The nominal phrase stands in apposition/predication to ἐγώ.
syn.rhet: The sequence culminates in royal sovereignty.

src: ἐγὼ ὑμᾶς ἀναστήσω διὰ τῆς ἐμῆς δεξιᾶς"
ana: ἐγὼ ὑμᾶς ἀναστήσω διὰ τῆς ἐμῆς δεξιᾶς·
lit: I will raise you up through my right hand;
sm: I will raise you up by my right hand;

syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Verb: ἀναστήσω. Object: ὑμᾶς. Instrumental phrase: διὰ τῆς ἐμῆς δεξιᾶς.
syn.rhet: Royal identity turns into direct eschatological promise.

src: ἐγὼ ὑμᾶς ἀνάγω εἰς τὰ ὑψηλὰ τῶν οὐρανῶν"
ana: ἐγὼ ὑμᾶς ἀνάγω εἰς τὰ ὑψηλὰ τῶν οὐρανῶν·
lit: I lead you up into the heights of the heavens;
sm: I lead you up to the heights of heaven;

syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Verb: ἀνάγω. Object: ὑμᾶς. Goal phrase: εἰς τὰ ὑψηλὰ τῶν οὐρανῶν.
syn.rhet: The speaker promises not only resurrection but exaltation.

src: ἐκεῖ ὑμῖν δείξω τὸν ἀπ᾽ αἰώνων πατέρα.
lit: There I will show you the Father from the ages.
sm: There I will show you the Father who is from all ages.

syn.mc: Main promise clause concluding the invitation section.
syn.svo: Adverb: ἐκεῖ. Dative indirect object: ὑμῖν. Verb: δείξω. Object: τὸν ἀπ᾽ αἰώνων πατέρα.
syn.rhet: The promise culminates in revelation of the eternal Father.

src: ὗτός ἐστιν ὁ ποιήσας τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ τὴν γῆν
ana: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ ποιήσας τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ τὴν γῆν
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: This is the one having made heaven and earth
sm: This is the one who made heaven and earth,

syn.mc: Main identificatory clause starting a final christological encomium.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate/appositional phrase: ὁ ποιήσας τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ τὴν γῆν.
syn.ptc: ποιήσας is substantival with the article.
syn.rhet: The closing section expands Christ's identity to cosmic creator.

src: καὶ πλάσας ἐν ἀρχῇ τὸν ἄνθρωπον,
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: And having formed in the beginning the human being,
sm: and who formed humanity in the beginning,

syn.mc: Coordinate participial member continuing the identification begun in line 841.
syn.svo: Participle: πλάσας. Object: τὸν ἄνθρωπον. Temporal phrase: ἐν ἀρχῇ.
syn.ptc: πλάσας is appositional to the subject οὗτός.
syn.rhet: Creation narrows from cosmos to humanity.

src: ὁ διὰ νόμου καὶ προφητῶν κηρυσσόμενος,
tok: pres mp ptcp nom sg m
lit: The one being proclaimed through law and prophets,
sm: the one proclaimed through the Law and the Prophets,

syn.mc: Articular participial apposition continuing the identification.
syn.svo: Articular participial phrase: ὁ ... κηρυσσόμενος, with means phrase διὰ νόμου καὶ προφητῶν.
syn.ptc: κηρυσσόμενος is substantival and appositional to the same referent.
syn.app: This articular participle further names the subject introduced at line 841.
syn.rhet: Scriptural testimony bridges creation and incarnation.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: The one made flesh in a virgin,
sm: the one incarnate through a virgin,
emd: The raw ὃ is corrected to ὁ to restore the articular participle series.

syn.mc: Articular participial apposition continuing the identification.
syn.svo: Articular participial phrase: ὁ ... σαρκωθείς, with prepositional phrase ἐπὶ παρθένῳ.
syn.ptc: σαρκωθείς continues the appositional participial chain.
syn.app: The clause identifies the subject by incarnation.
syn.rhet: The creator-proclaimed one is now named in incarnational terms.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: The one hanged on a tree,
sm: the one hung upon the tree,

syn.mc: Articular participial apposition continuing the same referent.
syn.svo: Articular participial phrase: ὁ ... κρεμασθείς with locative phrase ἐπὶ ξύλῳ.
syn.ptc: κρεμασθείς is appositional, moving the catalogue into the passion.
syn.rhet: The incarnation sequence gives way to crucifixion imagery.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: The one buried into the earth,
sm: the one laid in the earth,

syn.svo: Articular participial phrase: ὁ ... ταφείς with goal phrase εἰς γῆν.
syn.ptc: ταφείς continues the appositional chain through burial.
syn.rhet: The descent movement continues from tree to earth.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: The one raised from the dead
sm: the one raised from the dead,

syn.svo: Articular participial phrase: ὁ ... ἀνασταθείς with source phrase ἐκ νεκρῶν.
syn.ptc: ἀνασταθείς carries the chain into resurrection.
syn.rhet: The burial descent is immediately reversed by resurrection.

src: καὶ ἀνελθὼν εἰς τὰ ὑψηλὰ τῶν οὐρανῶν,
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: And having gone up into the heights of the heavens,
sm: and having ascended to the heights of heaven,

syn.svo: Participle: ἀνελθών. Goal phrase: εἰς τὰ ὑψηλὰ τῶν οὐρανῶν.
syn.rhet: Resurrection opens immediately into ascension.

src: ὁ καθήμενος ἐν δεξιᾷ τοῦ πατρός,
tok: pres mp ptcp nom sg m
lit: The one sitting at the right hand of the Father,
sm: the one seated at the Father's right hand,

syn.svo: Articular participial phrase: ὁ καθήμενος, with locative phrase ἐν δεξιᾷ τοῦ πατρός.
syn.ptc: καθήμενος marks the subject's current exalted state.
syn.rhet: The ascended one is enthroned beside the Father.

tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: The one having authority to save all things,
sm: the one who has authority to save all things,

syn.mc: Articular participial apposition with complementary infinitive.
syn.svo: Articular participle: ὁ ἔχων. Object: ἐξουσίαν. Complementary infinitive: σώζειν with object πάντα.
syn.ptc: ἔχων continues the substantive participial chain.
syn.rhet: Enthronement is paired with universal saving power.

src: δι᾽ οὗ ἐποίησεν ὃ πατὴρ τὰ ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς μέχρι αἰώνων.
ana: δι᾽ οὗ ἐποίησεν ὁ πατὴρ τὰ ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς μέχρι αἰώνων.
lit: Through whom the Father made the things from the beginning until the ages.
sm: through whom the Father has wrought all things from the beginning unto the ages.
emd: The raw ὃ before πατήρ is corrected to ὁ as the necessary article.

syn.mc: Relative clause referring back to the exalted Christ.
syn.sub: δι᾽ οὗ introduces a relative instrument clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ πατήρ. Verb: ἐποίησεν. Relative instrument: δι᾽ οὗ. Object: τὰ ... μέχρι αἰώνων.
syn.rhet: The encomium closes by attributing creation/history to the Father's action through the Son.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν τὸ ἃ καὶ τὸ ὥ:
ana: οὗτός ἐστιν τὸ α καὶ τὸ ω·
lit: This is the alpha and the omega;
sm: This is the Alpha and the Omega;
emd: The isolated letters are normalized from obvious OCR accent corruption: ἃ / ὥ -> α / ω.

syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate nominal: τὸ α καὶ τὸ ω.
syn.rhet: The close echoes Revelation's totalizing formula of origin and consummation.

lit: This is beginning and end,
sm: This is the beginning and the end,

syn.mc: Main identificatory clause parallel to line 853.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate nominals: ἀρχὴ καὶ τέλος.
syn.rhet: The alphabetic formula is unfolded into conceptual antithesis.

lit: Beginning indescribable and end incomprehensible;
sm: a beginning beyond telling and an end beyond comprehension;

syn.svo: No finite verb; ἐστιν is understood from line 854. Predicate nominals: ἀρχὴ ... καὶ τέλος ....
syn.rhet: Balanced epithets deepen the beginning/end paradox.

lit: This is the Christ;
sm: This is the Christ;
emd: Followed change_log.txt for the final punctuation ἢ -> ·, and corrected raw ὃ to ὁ as the required article.

syn.mc: Main identificatory clause opening the final acclamatory sequence.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate nominal: ὁ Χριστός.
syn.rhet: The metaphysical predicates collapse into the direct confession 'Christ'.

lit: This is the king;
sm: This is the king;

syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate nominal: ὁ βασιλεύς.
syn.rhet: The confession proceeds in short acclamatory strokes.

lit: This is Jesus;
sm: This is Jesus;
emd: Normalized stray spacing psili in ᾿Ιησοῦς -> Ἰησοῦς.

syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate nominal: Ἰησοῦς.
syn.rhet: The confession names the historical person directly.

lit: This one, the commander;
sm: This is the commander;
emd: Followed change_log.txt note: editorial parentheses around (ὁ) are preserved.

syn.svo: Subject: οὗτος. Predicate nominal: (ὁ) στρατηγός, with ἐστιν understood.
syn.app: The parenthesized article is editorial and preserved.
syn.rhet: The confession adopts martial language after the victory catalogue.

lit: This one, the Lord;
sm: This is the Lord;

syn.svo: Subject: οὗτος. Predicate nominal: ὁ κύριος, with ἐστιν understood.
syn.rhet: Another clipped acclamation intensifies the litany.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: This one, the one having risen from the dead;
sm: This is the one risen from the dead;
emd: The raw ὃ is corrected to ὁ to restore the articular participial phrase.

syn.svo: Subject: οὗτος. Predicate nominal: ὁ ἀναστὰς ἐκ νεκρῶν, with ἐστιν understood.
syn.ptc: ἀναστάς is substantival with the article.
syn.rhet: The litany returns to resurrection as defining identity.

src: οὗτος ὁ καθήμενος ἐν δεξιᾷ τοῦ πατρός"
ana: οὗτος ὁ καθήμενος ἐν δεξιᾷ τοῦ πατρός·
tok: pres mp ptcp nom sg m
lit: This one, the one sitting at the right hand of the Father;
sm: This is the one seated at the Father's right hand;

syn.svo: Subject: οὗτος. Predicate nominal: ὁ καθήμενος ἐν δεξιᾷ τοῦ πατρός, with ἐστιν understood.
syn.ptc: καθήμενος is substantival with the article.
syn.rhet: The confession pairs resurrection with enthronement.

src: φορεῖ τὸν πατέρα καὶ ὑπὸ τοῦ πατρὸς φορεῖται"
ana: φορεῖ τὸν πατέρα καὶ ὑπὸ τοῦ πατρὸς φορεῖται·
lit: He bears the Father and is borne by the Father;
sm: He bears the Father and is himself borne by the Father;

syn.mc: Paradoxical main clause closing the acclamatory litany.
syn.svo: Subject: implied οὗτος / Christ. Verbs: φορεῖ, φορεῖται. Object of φορεῖ: τὸν πατέρα. Agent phrase of φορεῖται: ὑπὸ τοῦ πατρός.
syn.rhet: A paradox of mutual indwelling crowns the christological climax.

src: ᾧ ἡ δόξα καὶ τὸ κράτος εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας. ἀμήν.
lit: To whom the glory and the dominion unto the ages. Amen.
sm: To him be glory and dominion unto the ages. Amen.

syn.sub: The relative pronoun refers back to Christ.
syn.svo: Dative referent: ᾧ. Subjects/predicate nominals: ἡ δόξα καὶ τὸ κράτος. Implied predicate such as ἔστω. Prepositional phrase: εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας.
syn.rhet: The work closes in formal doxology.

lit: Of Melito, On Passover.
sm: Melito, On the Passover.

syn.svo: Genitive of authorship: Μελίτωνος. Title phrase: Περὶ πάσχα.
syn.rhet: The subscription formally closes the text.
